---
title: "Schedule & Materials"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---

## Week 1: Intro to R and Working with Data

**Readings:** [R for Data Science, Intro](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/intro.html)

- [In-class activity](Lecture_01/In Class 1-13.qmd)

## Week 2: Data Visualization & Intro to Probability

**Readings:**

- [R for Data Science, Chapters 1--5](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/)
- [Probability!, Chapters 1--2](https://bookdown.org/probability/beta)

**Materials:**

- [Slides: Data Viz & Probability](Lecture_03/PADM8410_DataViz_Probability.qmd)
- [In-class activity](Lecture_02/in class.qmd)

## Week 3: Describing Variables

**Readings:**

- [Probability!, Chapters 3--4](https://bookdown.org/probability/beta)
- [The Effect, Chapter 3](https://theeffectbook.net/)

**Materials:**

- [Slides: Distributions](Lecture_03/PADM8410_Distributions.qmd)

## Week 4: Relationships Between Variables

**Readings:**

- [Probability!, Chapters 6--7](https://bookdown.org/probability/beta)
- [The Effect, Chapter 4](https://theeffectbook.net/)

**Materials:**

- [Slides](PADM8410Lecture4_Revised.qmd)

## Week 5: Sampling and Estimation

**Optional readings:**

- [Gerber, Green, and Larimer (2008), "Social Pressure and Voter Turnout"](https://doi.org/10.1017/S000305540808009X)

**Materials:**

- [Slides](PADM8410Lecture5_Revised.qmd)

## Week 6: Hypothesis Testing, P-values & Confidence Intervals

**Readings:** Gelman, Chapters 13--15

**Materials:**

- [Slides](PADM8410Lecture6_Revised.qmd)

## Week 7: OLS Regression

**Readings:** Huntington-Klein, Chapter 13 *or* Gelman, Chapters 6--8

**Materials:**

- [Slides](PADM8410Lecture7_Regression.qmd)

## Week 8: Regression Deep Dive

**Materials:**

- [Slides](PADM8410Lecture8_Regression_Deep_Dive.qmd)

## Week 9: MLE / GLM (Other Types of Regression)

**Readings:** Gelman, Chapters 13--15

**Optional readings:**

- [Kerevel (2015), "Paving the Way to the Ballot Box: Mexican Women's Legal Right to Vote"](https://doi.org/10.1177/1065912915585482)
- [Bolton and Thrower (2016), "Legislative Capacity and Executive Unilateralism"](https://doi.org/10.1111/ajps.12189)

**Materials:**

- [Slides](PADM8410Lecture9_MLE.qmd)

## Week 10: Introduction to Causal Inference

**Readings:** [The Effect, Chapters 5--8](https://theeffectbook.net/)

**Optional readings:**

- [Holland (1986), "Statistics and Causal Inference"](https://doi.org/10.2307/2289064)
- [Pager (2003), "The Mark of a Criminal Record"](https://doi.org/10.1086/374403)
- [Angrist and Evans (1998), "Children and Their Parents' Labor Supply"](https://doi.org/10.1257/aer.88.3.450)
- [Lundberg, Johnson, and Stewart (2021), "What Is Your Estimand?"](https://doi.org/10.1177/00031224211004187)
- [Manski (1994), "The Selection Problem"](https://doi.org/10.1007/978-94-011-0188-5_7)

**Materials:**

- [Slides](PADM8410Lecture10_CausalInference.qmd)

## Week 11: Randomized Controlled Trials

**Readings:** [The Effect, Chapter 10](https://theeffectbook.net/)

**Optional readings:**

- [Brandt (1978), "Racism and Research: The Case of the Tuskegee Syphilis Study"](https://doi.org/10.2307/3561468)
- [Milgram (1963), "Behavioral Study of Obedience"](https://doi.org/10.1037/h0040525)
- [Petrosino et al. (2013), "Scared Straight and Other Juvenile Awareness Programs for Preventing Juvenile Delinquency: A Systematic Review"](https://doi.org/10.4073/csr.2013.5)
- [Duflo, Hanna, and Ryan (2012), "Incentives Work: Getting Teachers to Come to School"](https://doi.org/10.1257/aer.102.4.1241)
- [Hainmueller, Hopkins, and Yamamoto (2014), "Causal Inference in Conjoint Analysis"](https://doi.org/10.1093/pan/mpt024)

**Materials:**

- [Slides](PADM8410Lecture11_Experiments.qmd)

## Week 12: Panel Data & Fixed Effects

**Readings:**

- [The Effect, Chapter 15](https://theeffectbook.net/)
- [Causal Inference: The Mixtape, Chapter 8](https://mixtape.scunning.com/08-panel_data)

**Optional readings:**

- [Ren (2025), "Time-Invariant Variables' Time-Varying Effects: Misinterpretations of the Fixed-Effects Model"](https://doi.org/10.1111/soc4.70113)
- [Abadie et al. (2023), "When Should You Adjust Standard Errors for Clustering?"](https://doi.org/10.1093/qje/qjac038)
- [Kubinec, "Understanding Two-Way Fixed Effects" (blog post)](https://www.robertkubinec.com/post/fixed_effects/)

**Materials:**

- [Slides](PADM8410Lecture12_FixedEffects.qmd)

## Week 13: Difference-in-Differences

**Readings:**

- [The Effect, Chapter 18](https://theeffectbook.net/ch-DifferenceinDifference.html)
- [Causal Inference: The Mixtape, Chapter 9](https://mixtape.scunning.com/09-difference_in_differences)

**Optional readings:**

- [Card and Krueger (1994), "Minimum Wages and Employment"](https://doi.org/10.1257/aer.90.5.1397)
- [Goodman-Bacon (2021), "Difference-in-Differences with Variation in Treatment Timing"](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2021.03.014)
- [Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021), "Difference-in-Differences with Multiple Time Periods"](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.12.001)

**Materials:**

- [Slides](PADM8410Lecture13_DiD.qmd)

## Week 14: Regression Discontinuity

**Readings:**

- [The Effect, Chapter 20](https://theeffectbook.net/)
- [Causal Inference: The Mixtape, Chapter 6](https://mixtape.scunning.com/06-regression_discontinuity)

**Optional readings:**

- [Eyting et al. (2025), "Shingles Vaccination and the Risk of Dementia"](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-025-08800-x)
- [Card, Dobkin, and Maestas (2008), "The Impact of Nearly Universal Insurance Coverage on Health Care Utilization"](https://doi.org/10.1257/aer.98.5.2242)
- [Angrist and Lavy (1999), "Using Maimonides' Rule to Estimate the Effect of Class Size"](https://doi.org/10.1162/003355399556061)
- [Lee and Lemieux (2010), "Regression Discontinuity Designs in Economics"](https://doi.org/10.1257/jel.48.2.281)
- [Cattaneo, Idrobo, and Titiunik (2020), *A Practical Introduction to Regression Discontinuity Designs*](https://rdpackages.github.io/references/Cattaneo-Idrobo-Titiunik_2020_CUP.pdf)

**Materials:**

- [Slides](PADM8410Lecture14_RDD.qmd)
